response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack and systems software engineer with over 16 years of experience, but his background is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The Principal Performance Architect position requires deep hardware expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator design, SIMD/MIMD architecture, GPU workload analysis, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His experience is concentrated in web technologies, application-layer performance, and developer tooling. While he has done some low-latency software work and recent AI agent infrastructure contributions, these do not translate to the hardware-level expertise this role demands.
strengths: 16+ years of broad software engineering experience, including low-latency, high-throughput systems handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby
Recent hands-on work with AI agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq, showing exposure to AI tooling ecosystems
Demonstrated performance optimization mindset through async I/O, caching, and fiber-based concurrency in DLang
concerns: Zero experience with SoC or IP modeling, which is the core technical requirement of this role
No hardware architecture background — no mention of GPU, SIMD/MIMD, AI accelerators, deep learning workload optimization, or hardware-software co-design anywhere in the resume

Educational background (BS in Computer Science + BS in Photography/Fine Arts) and entire career trajectory are oriented toward software applications, not the chip/system architecture domain this role requires

key_factors.factor: No hardware architecture or GPU/accelerator expertise
